mainEntity.name: We barely have any content—can we still get cited by voice assistants, or do we need a huge site first?

acceptedAnswer.text:
If you don’t have much content yet, start by building a focused FAQ and a few in‑depth guides around your most important topics. Voice assistants need something substantial to quote; thin or placeholder pages almost never get cited.

Quality beats quantity: a few well‑structured, authoritative pages can outperform a large but shallow site.

KEY FACTORS
- Identify 10–20 real questions customers ask and answer them clearly
- Create 2–3 pillar pages with deep, well‑organized content
- Use question‑style subheadings and short direct answers under each
- Add FAQPage and relevant schema to make everything machine‑legible

Don’t rush to publish dozens of weak pages; concentrate your effort into content that could plausibly earn snippets and AI citations. Expand only after those perform.

This builds a strong foundation that voice assistants can confidently pull from as your site grows.
acceptedAnswer.description: You don’t need a huge site, but you do need strong content. Start with a focused FAQ and a few deep, well‑structured guides that use question headings, short answers, and schema. A small set of high‑quality pages can absolutely earn voice citations if they clearly answer important queries.
